brief_justification: Agent calculated 54 hours via multiplicative factor combination but the correct answer is 22 hours, indicating a misunderstanding of how the factors interact — wrong answer but the arithmetic is internally consistent; Agent broke down each factor individually and explained why three other options fail, going one level beyond surface calculation; Agent noted graceful degradation on API timeout but never questioned whether treating all three factors as independent multipliers was the correct modeling choice; Agent cross-validated by auditing all four options and exposing each option's implied math, though no independent re-derivation from a different angle was attempted; Agent presented 54 hours as the definitive answer with no hedging about whether factor interactions could be interpreted differently or whether factors might partially share causality; Agent dismissed option A as simply ignoring all three factors without genuinely evaluating the possibility that the processing and validation overheads are already embedded in the 60% utilization figure; Every arithmetic step is explicitly written out with units and intermediate values, fully reproducible by a third party; Agent's conservative overestimate (54 hours vs. 22-hour base) directly opposes the best-case anchoring anti-pattern, though the avoidance is implicit rather than named; Agent avoided optimism bias and best-case anchoring by computing a higher figure, but no suppressed failure modes were explicitly called out; Agent applied a strictly linear sequence of multiplications rather than following the topology's conditional branches, p90-comparison gate, and loop-back verification structure.
